message:
feat(supply_chain): add manually_adjusted flag and evidence_derived_level to CertificationResult (bd-1wpq)

When an operator manually promotes or demotes a certification level, the
previous implementation overwrote the level field but left no trace of
whether the current level was evidence-based or manually adjusted. This
made it impossible for downstream readers to detect the gap between
what the evidence supports and what was administratively set.

New fields on CertificationResult:
- manually_adjusted (bool): true when the level was set by a manual
  promotion/demotion rather than evidence-based evaluation
- evidence_derived_level (CertificationLevel): preserves the original
  evidence-backed level across manual adjustments; equals level for
  evidence-based evaluations

Implementation:
- evaluate_certification() sets manually_adjusted=false and
  evidence_derived_level=level for all evidence-based evaluations
- CertificationRegistry::adjust_level() (promotion/demotion path) now
  preserves evidence_derived_level before overwriting level, and sets
  manually_adjusted=true
- Idempotent: repeated manual adjustments preserve the original
  evidence_derived_level from the first evidence-based evaluation

Tests added/updated:
- test_evaluate_sets_consistent_evidence_derived_level
- test_promote_demote_preserves_evidence_derived_level_across_adjustments
- Assertions added to existing promotion/demotion tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
